find_correlated_metrics.description: Scans all metrics and ranks by Pearson correlation strength against the primary metric within a lookback window ending at the anomaly date. Answers: 'Which other metrics moved with the anomaly?'
find_correlated_metrics.returns: TABLE with metric_pair, primary_metric, correlated_metric, correlation, abs_correlation, direction, data_points, window_start, window_end

calculate_lag_correlation.description: Sweeps lag -N to +N for a metric pair and computes Pearson correlation at each lag. Negative lag = other_metric LEADS primary (causal candidate). Positive lag = other_metric LAGS primary.
calculate_lag_correlation.returns: TABLE with lag_periods, lag_direction, correlation, abs_correlation, interpretation
calculate_lag_correlation.example: SELECT * FROM calculate_lag_correlation('revenue', 'p99_latency_ms', '2024-01-15'::DATE, 14, 5) ORDER BY abs_correlation DESC;

decompose_impact_by_dimension.description: Breaks total anomaly impact into segment-level contributions. Compares anomaly period values against baseline average. contribution_to_total shows each segment as % of total delta.
parameters: p_baseline_days: Days before anomaly for baseline (INTEGER, default 7)
decompose_impact_by_dimension.returns: TABLE with dimension_value, baseline_avg, anomaly_actual, absolute_delta, pct_delta, segment_weight, contribution_to_total, impact_rank

build_anomaly_explanation_payload.description: Assembles complete structured JSONB payload for a detected anomaly including stats, correlated metrics, leading indicators (lag analysis), and dimensional decomposition for LLM explanation layer.
build_anomaly_explanation_payload.returns: JSONB payload with anomaly stats, correlations, leading_indicators, impact_by_segment

calculate_sma.description: Calculate Simple Moving Average with Bollinger-style bands. Returns SMA, deviation, and upper/lower bands (±2 std dev).
parameters: p_data: JSONB array of {time, value, group?}
calculate_sma.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, sma_value, deviation, percent_deviation, upper_band, lower_band

calculate_wma.description: Calculate Weighted Moving Average giving more weight to recent values. More responsive than SMA.
calculate_wma.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, wma_value, deviation, percent_deviation

calculate_moving_variance.description: Calculate moving variance, std dev, coefficient of variation, and Z-scores for volatility analysis.
calculate_moving_variance.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, moving_mean, moving_variance, moving_std, coefficient_variation, z_score

calculate_moving_quantiles.description: Calculate moving quartiles (Q1, median, Q3) and IQR. Robust to outliers.
calculate_moving_quantiles.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, q25, q50_median, q75, iqr

calculate_moving_minmax.description: Calculate moving min/max with range and position metrics. position_in_range: 0=at min, 1=at max.
calculate_moving_minmax.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, moving_min, moving_max, moving_range, position_in_range

calculate_moving_correlation.description: Calculate moving correlation between two time series. Identifies changing relationships over time.
parameters: p_data_x: JSONB array for series X
p_data_y: JSONB array for series Y
calculate_moving_correlation.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, value_x, value_y, correlation, correlation_strength

calculate_moving_sum.description: Calculate moving sum with contribution percentage. Shows each value's contribution to recent total.
calculate_moving_sum.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, moving_sum, contribution_pct

calculate_expanding_window.description: Calculate metrics using all prior data (expanding window). Operations: mean, sum, std, min, max, count.
calculate_expanding_window.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, expanding_value, window_size

calculate_cumulative_operations.description: Calculate multiple cumulative operations: sum, product, max, min. Returns percent_of_total for cumulative sum.
calculate_cumulative_operations.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, cumsum, cumproduct, cummax, cummin, percent_of_total

calculate_time_weighted_ma.description: Calculate time-weighted moving average with exponential decay. More weight on recent observations.
calculate_time_weighted_ma.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, twma_value, deviation

calculate_bollinger_bands.description: Calculate Bollinger Bands: SMA with ±N standard deviation bands. Returns bandwidth and %B (position within bands).
parameters: p_num_std: Number of std devs (DECIMAL, default 2.0)
calculate_bollinger_bands.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, middle_band, upper_band, lower_band, bandwidth, percent_b

calculate_moving_rank.description: Calculate moving rank and percentile rank within window. Identifies relative position of values.
calculate_moving_rank.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, window_rank, window_percentile, is_highest, is_lowest

calculate_percent_change_comparison.description: Calculate percent change between treatment and baseline groups. Returns relative uplift and absolute change for A/B test analysis.
calculate_percent_change_comparison.returns: TABLE with condition_value, metric_name, baseline_avg, treatment_avg, absolute_change, percent_change, relative_uplift

calculate_absolute_change_comparison.description: Calculate absolute change with standard errors and z-scores for statistical significance testing.
calculate_absolute_change_comparison.returns: TABLE with condition_value, metric_name, baseline_avg, treatment_avg, absolute_diff, std_error, z_score

calculate_prepost_comparison.description: Pre-post analysis for within-subject comparisons. Automatically determines cutoff time if not provided.
calculate_prepost_comparison.returns: TABLE with entity_id, pre_value, post_value, absolute_change, percent_change, change_direction, change_magnitude

calculate_stratified_analysis.description: Stratified analysis (Mantel-Haenszel style) for confounding adjustment. Calculates stratum-specific effects with weights.
calculate_stratified_analysis.returns: TABLE with stratum_value, treatment_mean, control_mean, stratum_effect, stratum_weight, stratum_n

calculate_bootstrap_ci.description: Bootstrap confidence intervals for robust inference. Supports mean, median, and standard deviation.
calculate_bootstrap_ci.returns: TABLE with metric_type, point_estimate, ci_lower, ci_upper, confidence_level, sample_size

calculate_power_analysis.description: Sample size calculation for desired statistical power. Returns required sample size per group and Cohen d.
calculate_power_analysis.returns: TABLE with effect_size, std_deviation, alpha_level, target_power, required_sample_per_group, total_sample_required, cohens_d

calculate_effect_sizes.description: Calculate multiple effect size measures: Cohen d, Hedges g, Glass delta. Provides interpretation (negligible/small/medium/large).
parameters: p_data_treatment: JSONB array for treatment group
p_data_control: JSONB array for control group
calculate_effect_sizes.returns: TABLE with effect_size_type, effect_size_value, interpretation, treatment_mean, control_mean, pooled_std

adjust_pvalues_bonferroni.description: Bonferroni correction for multiple comparison adjustment. Prevents false positives in multiple hypothesis testing.
adjust_pvalues_bonferroni.returns: TABLE with comparison_number, original_pvalue, adjusted_pvalue, is_significant_original, is_significant_adjusted, bonferroni_correction

calculate_sequential_analysis.description: Sequential analysis for A/B test monitoring with early stopping. Implements O'Brien-Fleming boundary (simplified).
calculate_sequential_analysis.returns: TABLE with sample_size, treatment_mean, control_mean, effect_size, z_score, p_value, is_significant, stopping_boundary, recommendation

calculate_cuped_adjustment.description: CUPED (Controlled-experiment Using Pre-Experiment Data) adjustment. Reduces variance using pre-experiment covariates.
calculate_cuped_adjustment.returns: TABLE with treatment_group, unadjusted_mean, adjusted_mean, variance_reduction, cuped_theta

calculate_lag.description: Calculate lagged values (shift backward in time) with change metrics. Supports grouping for panel data.
calculate_lag.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, lagged_value, absolute_change, percent_change, lag_periods

calculate_lead.description: Calculate lead values (shift forward in time) for predictive analysis. Useful for forward-looking features.
calculate_lead.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, lead_value, absolute_change, percent_change, lead_periods

analyze_variance.description: Analyze variance using rolling, expanding, or exponential windows. Returns variance, std dev, CV, and Z-scores.
analyze_variance.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, variance, std_dev, coefficient_variation, window_mean, z_score

analyze_distribution.description: Comprehensive distribution analysis with quartiles, skewness, and kurtosis. Supports grouped analysis.
analyze_distribution.returns: TABLE with group_name, count_values, mean_value, median_value, mode_value, std_dev, variance, min_value, max_value, range_value, q1, q3, iqr, skewness, kurtosis

calculate_difference.description: Calculate first and second-order differences for stationarity. Tests whether differenced series is stationary.
parameters: p_order: 1 for first difference, 2 for second (INTEGER, default 1)
calculate_difference.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, first_difference, second_difference, is_stationary

calculate_cdf.description: Calculate empirical cumulative distribution function. Returns percentile ranks and cumulative probabilities.
calculate_cdf.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, cdf_value, percentile_rank, cumulative_count, group_name

calculate_rolling_window.description: General-purpose rolling window with multiple aggregations. Supports mean, sum, min, max, std, count.
calculate_rolling_window.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, rolling_value, deviation_from_rolling, percent_deviation, window_size

calculate_ema.description: Exponential moving average with configurable smoothing factor. Alpha closer to 1 = more responsive, closer to 0 = smoother.
calculate_ema.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, ema_value, deviation

calculate_autocorrelation.description: Calculate autocorrelation function (ACF) up to specified lag. Tests significance using confidence bounds.
calculate_autocorrelation.returns: TABLE with lag_period, autocorrelation, is_significant, confidence_lower, confidence_upper

test_stationarity.description: Simplified stationarity test checking mean, variance, and trend stability. Returns actionable recommendations.
test_stationarity.returns: TABLE with test_name, is_stationary, mean_value, variance, trend_slope, recommendation

calculate_cumulative.description: Calculate cumulative operations: sum, product, max, min. Returns percent of total for cumulative sum. Input uses 'value' field.
calculate_cumulative.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, cumulative_value, percent_of_total

calculate_percent_change.description: Calculate period-over-period percent changes. Supports simple, log, and compound methods. Auto-categorizes magnitude.
calculate_percent_change.returns: TABLE with row_number, time_period, original_value, previous_value, absolute_change, percent_change, log_change, change_category

aggregate_by_time.description: Aggregate time series data into time periods (hour, day, week, month, quarter, year). Supports sum, avg, min, max, count, stddev.
aggregate_by_time.returns: TABLE with time_period, aggregated_value, record_count, min_value, max_value, avg_value, stddev_value

calculate_moving_average.description: Calculate moving averages: Simple (SMA), Weighted (WMA), or Exponential (EMA). Input uses 'metric' field. Detects deviations from trend.
calculate_moving_average.returns: TABLE with time_period, actual_value, ma_value, deviation, percent_deviation

calculate_growth_rates.description: Calculate period-over-period growth rates with annualization. Supports period_over_period, year_over_year, compound.
calculate_growth_rates.returns: TABLE with time_period, current_value, previous_value, absolute_change, percent_change, annualized_growth, growth_category

calculate_statistical_trend.description: Perform linear regression to identify statistical trends. Returns slope, R-squared, correlation, and significance testing.
calculate_statistical_trend.returns: TABLE with trend_direction, slope, intercept, r_squared, correlation, p_value, is_significant, data_points, trend_strength

forecast_linear.description: Generate linear forecasts with confidence intervals. Uses trend analysis to project future values.
forecast_linear.returns: TABLE with forecast_period, forecast_date, forecast_value, lower_bound, upper_bound, trend_component

calculate_volatility.description: Calculate rolling volatility metrics including std dev and coefficient of variation. Classifies: very_low, low, moderate, high, very_high.
calculate_volatility.returns: TABLE with time_period, metric_value, rolling_std, rolling_variance, coefficient_variation, volatility_score, volatility_level

compare_periods.description: Compare current period values with previous periods. Useful for MoM, QoQ, YoY analysis.
compare_periods.returns: TABLE with time_period, current_value, comparison_value, absolute_difference, relative_difference, comparison_period, change_direction

detect_seasonality.description: Detect seasonal patterns by grouping data into seasonal periods. Returns seasonal indices showing deviation from average.
parameters: p_season_length: Season length e.g. 12 for monthly yearly (INTEGER, default 12)
detect_seasonality.returns: TABLE with season_period, average_value, std_dev, min_value, max_value, coefficient_variation, seasonal_index

detect_anomalies.description: Detect statistical anomalies using Z-score or IQR methods. Flags high and low outliers with anomaly scores.
parameters: p_threshold_std: Std devs from mean (DECIMAL, default 2.0)
detect_anomalies.returns: TABLE with time_period, metric_value, is_anomaly, anomaly_score, deviation_from_mean, z_score, anomaly_type

get_top_metrics.description: Rank metrics by criteria: growth, volatility, absolute_value, trend_strength. Returns top N metrics.
parameters: p_metrics_data: JSONB object with metric_name keys and arrays of {time, value}
get_top_metrics.returns: TABLE with metric_name, ranking_score, latest_value, average_value, growth_rate, volatility, rank_position

calculate_cumulative_trend.description: Calculate cumulative values over time (sum, avg, max, min). From trend_analysis_functions - input uses 'metric' field. Returns cumulative percentages. Note: Same name as timeseries calculate_cumulative which uses 'value' field.
calculate_cumulative_trend.returns: TABLE with time_period, period_value, cumulative_value, cumulative_percent

classify_trend.description: Comprehensive trend classification with velocity and acceleration. Provides actionable recommendations.
classify_trend.returns: TABLE with overall_trend, trend_strength, direction_consistency, velocity, acceleration, recommendation

note: Likelihood and impact entries were removed from this vendored catalog; use custom SQL/services if needed. SQL examples are derived from COMMENT blocks and usage sections in each source file.
